summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 8: re-detect against main (55 bugs resolved/triaged rounds 0-4). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (61 sigs), adversarial verify. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 8 detection: 5 from clusters = 5 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 8 verify: 5 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Panel::authGuard() is dead config — login/logout/register and panel auth middleware always use the default guard, never Panel::getAuthGuard()
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/auth/src/Http/Requests/LoginRequest.php:53 (also LogoutController.php:22, RegisterController.php:46, Routes.php:60-175 middleware, EnsureUserCanAccessPanel.php:30) — none consume Panel::getAuthGuard() (packages/core/src/Panel/Panel.php:332)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Thread the panel's guard through the auth flow: resolve $guard = $panel->getAuthGuard() (or config('arqel.auth.guard')) and use Auth::guard($guard)->attempt(...) in LoginRequest, Auth::guard($guard)->login($user) in RegisterController, Auth::guard($guard)->logout() in LogoutController, and emit route middleware as "auth:{$guard}"/"guest:{$guard}" in Routes::register(); also have EnsureUserCanAccessPanel read $request->user($guard).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced by static inspection on main. grep confirms Panel::getAuthGuard() (Panel.php:332) has ZERO consumers outside Panel.php. Every auth entry point hardcodes the default guard: LoginRequest::authenticate uses Auth::attempt() (LoginRequest.php:53), RegisterController uses Auth::login() (RegisterController.php:46), LogoutController uses Auth::guard()->logout() with no name (LogoutController.php:22), Routes.php registers plain 'auth'/'guest'/'web' middleware with no :guard parameter (lines 60-175), and EnsureUserCanAccessPanel reads $request->user() from the default guard (line 30). The parallel config('arqel.auth.guard') (config/arqel.php:21) is also dead — only referenced in a stale, factually-wrong doc comment in the middleware. Crucially this is an ADVERTISED, DOCUMENTED public API: apps/docs Fluent API table lists authGuard(string) as 'Alternative guard (default web)', PLANNING/05-api-php.md:555 shows ->authGuard('admin') as a working example, and PLANNING/08-fase-1-mvp.md:639 declares it in the Phase-1 Panel contract. It is NOT a deferred feature (unlike tenant(), which docs explicitly mark 'Phase 2'). Consequence: a panel configured with ->authGuard('admin') on a custom guard silently authenticates/authorizes/registers/logs-out against the WRONG default 'web' guard — login appears to no-op for the intended guard, logout never clears its session, and panel gating inspects the wrong user. Classified framework-bug (broken builder method, not app misuse). Severity high rather than blocker: the default single-'web'-guard path (which showcase and most apps use) works correctly, so impact is confined to users who opt into a non-default custom guard — but for them it is a silent auth-correctness/security failure of a documented promise.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence:
Panel exposes a configurable auth guard: packages/core/src/Panel/Panel.php:48 `private string $authGuard = 'web'`, with the fluent setter `authGuard(string $guard)` (line 255) and accessor `getAuthGuard()` (line 332). But NO code anywhere consumes it: `grep -rn getAuthGuard packages/*/src` (excluding Panel.php) returns zero hits. Every auth controller hardcodes the default guard instead:
- LogoutController::__invoke (packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/LogoutController.php:22) calls `Auth::guard()->logout()` with no guard name.
- LoginRequest::authenticate (packages/auth/src/Http/Requests/LoginRequest.php:53) calls `Auth::attempt($credentials, ...)` (default guard).
- RegisterController::__invoke (packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/RegisterController.php:46) calls `Auth::login($user)` (default guard).
The routes (packages/auth/src/Routes.php) also apply plain `'auth'`/`'guest'` middleware with no `:guard` parameter, so route protection resolves the default web guard too. Consequence: a panel configured with `->authGuard('admin')` (a custom guard) silently authenticates, authorizes, registers, and logs out against the WRONG (default 'web') guard — login appears to do nothing for that guard, logout never clears the intended guard's session, and EnsureUserCanAccessPanel reads `$request->user()` from the default guard. The authGuard() builder API is non-functional.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Row actions with a record-dependent url(Closure) produce one broken URL shared by every row
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:208 (serializeMany row actions once with null record -> callToArray :506 passes $record=null); per-record path serializeRecord :550 only emits visible action names, never per-record url/disabled. Frontend ArqelIndexPage.tsx:59-60 only substitutes the {id} token.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Serialize row actions per-record: in serializeRecord(), attach a per-row resolved actions array by calling each action's toArray($user, $record, $resource) (so closure URLs and disabled(Closure) resolve against the real record), and have the frontend read the per-row action list instead of the single table-level one. Alternatively, make closure-URL row actions emit an {id}-templated URL at table level — but that loses arbitrary closure semantics, so per-record serialization is the correct fix.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced concretely. Row actions are serialized ONCE at table level: buildTableIndexData() line 208 calls serializeMany($rowActions, $user, $resource), which routes through callToArray() line 490-516 -> for Action-shaped objects (3+ params) calls $item->toArray($user, null, $resource) with $record=null (line 506). For a custom closure URL, Action::resolveUrl(null) (Action.php:256-268) runs the closure with null. PHP repro: RowAction::make('view')->url(fn($r)=>"/posts/{$r['id']}")->toArray(null,null,null) yields url '/posts/' (plus a 'Trying to access array offset on null' warning) — a single broken URL shipped to ALL rows. The frontend (ArqelIndexPage.tsx:59-60) only knows how to substitute the literal {id} token; stock actions emit '/admin/posts/{id}' (confirmed via repro) so they work, but a closure URL contains no {id} token, so every row links to the same broken URL. Per-record serializeRecord() (line 528-554) only computes visible action NAMES (resolveVisibleActionNames, line 550), never per-record URLs, so there is no per-row URL path. Same root cause breaks disabled(Closure): isDisabledFor(null) evaluated once at line 319. This is the documented, intended public API — packages/actions/SKILL.md:165-167 shows exactly RowAction::make('view_on_site')->url(fn($post)=>route('public.posts.show',$post),'GET') as a supported pattern, and ActionTest.php:53-56 unit-tests resolveUrl(['id'=>42]) with a record — but the gap is uncaught because it's never exercised end-to-end through serialization. Classified framework-bug (not app-misuse): the consumer uses the documented API exactly as specified; the framework's serialization layer fails to pass the record. Not a deferred feature. Severity medium: closure-URL/disabled-closure row actions are broken, but stock view/edit/delete/restore (the common case) work fine via the {id} template, limiting blast radius.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: InertiaDataBuilder::serializeMany serializes row actions ONCE at table level via callToArray, which calls Action::toArray($user, null, $resource) with $record=null (packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:208 -> :506). For a stock verb the URL is templated as {id} (Action::resolveStockUrl emits '{id}' when record is null, packages/actions/src/Action.php:351), and the frontend substitutes it (packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelIndexPage.tsx:59-60 `action.url.replace('{id}', String(recordId))`). But a custom record-dependent URL closure is resolved via Action::resolveUrl(null) (Action.php:256-268): the closure runs with $record=null. E.g. RowAction::make('view')->url(fn($r)=>"/posts/{$r['id']}") -> $r['id'] on null -> '/posts/' with no id. The single resolved URL is shipped to the client for ALL rows, and the frontend has no {id} token to substitute, so every row links to the same broken URL. Action closure-URL resolution IS tested in isolation with a record (actions/tests/Unit/ActionTest.php:53-56 resolveUrl(['id'=>42])) but never end-to-end through serialization, so the gap is uncaught. Same root cause also breaks per-row disabled(Closure): isDisabledFor(null) is evaluated once (Action.php:319).

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Sortable RelationshipColumn yields invalid SQL (orderBy on relation name, no JOIN) despite docblock promising JOIN wiring
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/table/src/TableQueryBuilder.php:90-108 (applySort) and :66-70 (applySearch); enabled by RelationshipColumn inheriting Column::sortable()/searchable() and the false-promise docblock at packages/table/src/Columns/RelationshipColumn.php:16-18
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In applySort/applySearch, special-case RelationshipColumn: either add a leftJoin on the related table and orderBy/where the resolved `getDisplayAttribute()` (e.g. authors.name), or — minimal/safe fix — exclude RelationshipColumns from getSortableColumnNames()/getSearchableColumnNames() and have RelationshipColumn override sortable()/searchable() to no-op (or throw) plus correct the docblock; full JOIN support is TABLE-005.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced by code trace on current main. RelationshipColumn extends TextColumn->Column and inherits sortable()/searchable() with no override (TextColumn only adds limit/wrap/fontFamily). A RelationshipColumn's name is the RELATION name (e.g. 'author'); the actual display column lives separately in $displayAttribute. getSortableColumnNames() (TableQueryBuilder.php:166-177) collects getName() for every sortable column indiscriminately, so 'author' passes the whitelist; applySort() then runs $this->query->orderBy('author', $direction) (line 107) with no JOIN. 'author' is a relation accessor, not a DB column, so Eloquent emits SQLSTATE[42S22] Unknown column 'author' -> 500. applySearch() (66-70) has the identical defect via orWhere('author','LIKE',...). I confirmed NO join/leftJoin wiring exists anywhere in packages/table/src (grep found only the misleading docblock and an unrelated QueryBuilderFilter comment). The RelationshipColumn docblock (lines 16-18) explicitly promises 'Sortable relationship columns require a JOIN; that wiring lives in TableQueryBuilder (TABLE-005)', so this is an unfulfilled documented contract, not an intended/deferred limitation. The broken path is untested: the only RelationshipColumn test (TableQueryBuilderTest.php:156-168) builds them without sortable()/searchable(). Severity medium not high/blocker: it requires the user to opt into ->sortable()/->searchable() on a RelationshipColumn — apps/showcase never uses RelationshipColumn (its PostResource has a belongsTo author but renders no author column), so no baseline crash — but any consumer following the docblock hits a hard SQL error. Could not refute.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: RelationshipColumn inherits sortable() from Column (packages/table/src/Column.php:92) and its name is the relation name (e.g. 'author'), display attribute separate (packages/table/src/Columns/RelationshipColumn.php:24,44). TableQueryBuilder::applySort whitelists the column then does plain $this->query->orderBy('author', $direction) with no join (packages/table/src/TableQueryBuilder.php:90-108). 'author' is not a DB column -> SQL error (unknown column). RelationshipColumn's own docblock claims 'Sortable relationship columns require a JOIN; that wiring lives in TableQueryBuilder (TABLE-005)' but applySort implements no JOIN. The eager-load test marks RelationshipColumns non-sortable (table/tests/Unit/TableQueryBuilderTest.php:155-166), so the path is untested. Searchable RelationshipColumns hit the same: applySearch does orWhere('author','LIKE',...) on a nonexistent column (TableQueryBuilder.php:66-70).

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: FileField/ImageField upload never applies the field's configured visibility() to the stored object
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Pass the field's storage visibility into the store options: `$stored = $upload->store($directory, ['disk' => $disk, 'visibility' => $fieldInstance->getVisibility()]);` (or call `storePublicly()` when getVisibility() === 'public').
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed at code level and could not refute. FileField exposes visibility('public'|'private') as a public, spec-documented API (packages/fields/src/Types/FileField.php:69-74,127; PLANNING/05-api-php.md:369 advertises ->visibility('public')) and stores the default 'private'. The upload controller's store() persists via $upload->store($directory, ['disk' => $disk]) at FieldUploadController.php:72 and never reads getVisibility(). With only the disk passed, Laravel applies the disk's DEFAULT visibility, so a field declaring visibility('public') on a non-public disk (e.g. s3 default private) is stored privately and the generated url() (lines 79-85) returns an inaccessible link; conversely a 'private' declaration on a public-default disk would leak the object. I grepped the entire fields/ and core/ src: getVisibility() is consumed nowhere except getTypeSpecificProps() serialization (line 184) — the 'visibility' references in core (FieldSchemaSerializer / InertiaDataBuilder) are the UNRELATED table/form display-visibility concept (a {table, form} array), not the filesystem ACL. So storage visibility is wired to React props but never to the actual store() call. This is a framework bug: the public API contract is documented (05-api-php.md:369) yet the implementation drops it. NOT app-misuse (the app calls ->visibility() exactly as advertised). NOT a deferred feature (the FileField upload pipeline shipped and is tested). Severity low: it only manifests on disks whose default visibility differs from the declared value; the default 'local' disk used by tests (Storage::fake('local')) and apps/showcase masks it, which is why it slipped through — tests only assert config serialization (FieldUploadControllerTest uses local disk; FileFieldTest:32 checks props), never the stored object's visibility. Narrow blast radius but a genuine correctness gap (broken public URLs / potential ACL mismatch) in a public API. The note's hedge about 08:2691 omitting visibility is true but non-decisive since 05-api-php.md:369 documents the contract. Fix is a one-liner adding 'visibility' => $fieldInstance->getVisibility() to the store options.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: FieldUploadController::store stores via $upload->store($directory, ['disk' => $disk]) and never passes the field's visibility (packages/fields/src/Http/Controllers/FieldUploadController.php:72). FileField exposes visibility('public'/'private') as public API (packages/fields/src/Types/FileField.php:69-74,127) and the planning spec advertises ->visibility('public') (PLANNING/05-api-php.md:369). On a non-public disk (e.g. s3) a field declared visibility('public') is stored with the disk default (private), so the generated url() (FieldUploadController.php:79-85) returns an inaccessible link. Tests only assert the visibility CONFIG serialization, never the stored object's visibility (fields/tests/Feature/FieldUploadControllerTest.php uses local disk; tests/Unit/Types/FileFieldTest.php:32 only checks props). NOTE lower confidence: the spec's own reference store() snippet (PLANNING/08-fase-1-mvp.md:2691) also omits visibility, so this may be a spec-level omission rather than an implementation regression.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: MCP dispatch reports missing/invalid tool & prompt arguments as -32603 Internal error instead of -32602 Invalid params
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/mcp/src/McpServer.php:200-211 (generic Throwable catch); handlers throw plain InvalidArgumentException at packages/mcp/src/Prompts/MigrateFilamentResourcePrompt.php:71, packages/mcp/src/Prompts/ReviewResourcePrompt.php:64, packages/mcp/src/Tools/DescribeResourceTool.php:61
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In McpServer::handleRequest add a `catch (InvalidArgumentException $e)` branch before the generic Throwable catch that returns errorResponse with self::ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS (-32602); or have the handlers/prompts throw McpDispatchException(..., ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS) for argument validation.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced live against the showcase McpServer singleton (booted via apps/showcase/bootstrap/app.php on current branch). `prompts/get {name:'migrate_filament_resource', arguments:[]}` returns {"error":{"code":-32603,"message":"Internal error: 'filament_file' parameter is required..."}}. Same for `prompts/get review_resource` (resource_file) and `tools/call describe_resource` with missing slug -> -32603. By contrast, an unknown prompt/tool NAME returns -32602 in the same dispatcher (callTool L341, getPrompt L431, readResource L390 all throw McpDispatchException with ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS). The argument-validation handlers (MigrateFilamentResourcePrompt.php:71, ReviewResourcePrompt.php:64, DescribeResourceTool.php:61) throw plain InvalidArgumentException, which is swallowed by the generic catch(Throwable) at McpServer.php:200-211 and mapped to ERROR_INTERNAL (-32603). Per JSON-RPC 2.0 / MCP, missing or invalid required params are -32602 (Invalid params); -32603 is reserved for internal server faults. This is internally inconsistent (name-level params already use -32602) and causes a spec-conformant MCP client to mislabel a user-correctable bad-argument call as a server crash. Not documented/intended: the only -32603 test (McpServerTest.php:165) asserts -32603 for a genuine RuntimeException('kaboom') handler fault, which is correct; no test or PLANNING/13 spec mandates -32603 for argument validation, so this is an accidental fall-through, not designed behavior. Classified framework-bug (defect lives entirely in packages/mcp dispatcher + bundled handlers, not in app code). Severity low: error classification / spec-conformance issue only -- the call still fails cleanly with a descriptive message; no crash, data loss, or showcase functional breakage.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Live repro against the showcase McpServer singleton: `prompts/get {name: 'migrate_filament_resource'}` (no `filament_file` arg) returns `{"error":{"code":-32603,"message":"Internal error: 'filament_file' parameter is required and must be a non-empty string"}}`; same for `review_resource` (missing `resource_file`). The prompt generators (e.g. ReviewResourcePrompt::generate, packages/mcp/src/Prompts/ReviewResourcePrompt.php:63-64) and tool handlers (DescribeResourceTool::__invoke, packages/mcp/src/Tools/DescribeResourceTool.php:60-61) throw plain InvalidArgumentException. In McpServer::handleRequest (packages/mcp/src/McpServer.php:200-211) only McpDispatchException maps to its declared JSON-RPC code; every other Throwable is swallowed by the generic catch as ERROR_INTERNAL (-32603). This is internally inconsistent: the same dispatcher already returns ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS (-32602) for a missing/unknown tool NAME (callTool, line 341) and a missing/unknown prompt NAME (getPrompt, line 431). Per JSON-RPC 2.0 / MCP, malformed or missing required params are -32602 (Invalid params), not -32603 (Internal error). A spec-conformant MCP client distinguishes user-correctable argument errors from server faults by this code, so it mislabels a recoverable bad-argument call as a server crash. Fix: have handlers/prompts throw McpDispatchException(..., ERROR_INVALID_PARAMS) for argument validation, or have McpServer translate validation exceptions to -32602.
